title: Distributed-Trace Fanout, Span Depth, N+1 Query Patterns, and Runaway Microservice Chain Anti-Pattern Detection

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — seed itsi_apm_nplusone_antipattern_catalog.csv and episode analytics when service KPIs tie to trace-shape regressions

dataSources: index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces for Splunk APM span events with trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id, service.name, operation.name, http.method, http.url, db.system, db.statement, duration_ms, trace_depth; index=otel_spans for raw OTLP-normalized spans mirrored to Splunk Enterprise; index=metrics sourcetype in (apm:metricsets, otel_metric, prometheus:metrics) for per-trace span_count rollups aligned to Splunk APM MetricSets concepts; lookup itsi_apm_nplusone_antipattern_catalog.csv for ITSI N plus one pattern weights; lookup apm_endpoint_span_slo_inventory.csv for slo_max_span_count, owner_team, environment, cluster_tier per endpoint_key
app: Splunk Observability Cloud APM, Splunk APM on Splunk Platform, Splunk ITSI, Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector, Splunk OTel Collector Kubernetes Helm charts
description: Detects pathological distributed-trace shapes that signal architectural anti-patterns: trace fanout spikes from a single logical request materializing as more than about five hundred child spans consistent with object-relational mapper N plus one database access, span depth beyond twenty-five levels suggesting recursive or cyclic call graphs, endpoint-level span_count outliers against ninety-ninth percentile baselines, more than about fifteen distinct service.name values inside one trace_id indicating runaway microservice chains or over-decomposition, and duplicate-span clusters where span_count dwarfs distinct operation.name cardinality implying retries or double instrumentation. Surfaces db.system and db.statement context when present for relational suspicion without claiming duration or error-rate root cause.
value: Platform capacity and FinOps leaders see early warning when observability tax and ingest load climb because teams accidentally ship chatty traces, not only when latency percentiles move. Architecture governance gains evidence for service boundary reviews before microservice sprawl hardens. Database reliability partners receive prioritized ORM and batching remediation hooks tied to trace_id exemplars instead of anecdotal log quotes.
grandmaExplanation: We count how many steps one request fans out across your systems and how deep the call stack grows. When a single click quietly spawns hundreds of tiny database calls, an overly long chain of services, or suspicious repeats, we raise a clear signal so teams fix the design before bills and slowdowns grow.
implementation: Land Splunk APM apm:traces, OpenTelemetry index=otel_spans, per-trace metric rollups in index=metrics, itsi_apm_nplusone_antipattern_catalog.csv, and apm_endpoint_span_slo_inventory.csv with slo_max_span_count. Save observability_uc_13_5_6_trace_shape_fanout_depth every 15m earliest=-4h@h; page on page severity; route via owner_team. Tune fanout, depth, service-cardinality, duplicate-cluster, and percentile multipliers per tier.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability platform engineers, application architects, database reliability leads, and site reliability partners who are accountable for Splunk APM, Splunk Observability Cloud trace exports, OpenTelemetry Collector fleets, and the cost envelope for indexed span volume. This use case is deliberately about pathological distributed-trace shape, not end-to-end wall-clock duration outliers that UC-13.5.1 owns with percentile baselines and dwell logic, not error-rate-first paging that UC-13.5.2 owns when status semantics dominate, not orphan spans and broken parent links that UC-13.5.3 owns when completeness and propagation fidelity fail, not live dependency graph discovery and edge drift that UC-13.5.4 owns when adjacency matrices change, not log-to-trace correlation coverage that UC-13.5.5 owns when identifiers on log lines fail Splunk APM Related Content pivots, not request-rate error-duration saturation dashboards that UC-13.5.7 frames for RED, not browser Core Web Vitals that UC-13.5.9 owns for real user measurement, and not error-budget burn math that UC-13.5.16 owns for SLO policy. Here the evidence story is whether a single user-visible request materializes as a bushy trace with hundreds or thousands of near-identical child spans typical of object-relational mapper N plus one access patterns, whether span depth climbs beyond humane recursion or accidental cyclic call graphs, whether cardinality of distinct service.name values inside one trace_id signals microservice over-decomposition or dependency loops, whether duplicate clusters of spans with the same operation.name imply chatty retries or double instrumentation, and whether per-endpoint span_count tails exceed declared slo_max_span_count thresholds maintained in inventory lookups.

Contract you are enforcing: trace_id still groups one distributed transaction, span_id still identifies one timed operation, parent_span_id still stitches a directed tree when exporters preserve linkage, and semantic conventions still name service.name, operation.name, http.method, http.url, db.system, and db.statement when present. Splunk APM MetricSets and trace-derived rollups can publish span_count style aggregates per trace for cheaper joins against raw span floods. ITSI content packs and custom knowledge objects may ship itsi_apm_nplusone_antipattern_catalog.csv style seed lookups that fingerprint common database access signatures correlated with relational N plus one shapes; treat those rows as advisory weights, not automatic verdicts, and keep human review on severity mapping.

Privacy and compliance: db.statement text can contain literal predicates and customer identifiers if developers forget sanitization; restrict indexes, mask at the collector when counsel requires minimization, and never paste raw statements into executive slides. Role-based access control should limit index=apm, index=otel_spans, and index=metrics trace partitions to engineering identities with job need, segregate production from non-production with deployment.environment, and document retention that balances forensic replay against regulatory minimization.

Financial risk briefing: every additional thousand spans per minute across checkout-scale traffic multiplies ingest license, search concurrency, and Observability Cloud entitlement pressure even when latency looks acceptable on averages. Platform FinOps should read this control as a capacity and architecture hygiene signal, not only a developer ergonomics nicety.

Differentiation recap: trace shape, fanout count, depth anomalies, duplicate span clusters, service cardinality inside a single trace, endpoint-level span_count percentile outliers, ITSI N plus one pattern enrichment, and SLO max span thresholds—not duration percentiles alone, not error codes alone, not log correlation coverage alone, not service map topology drift alone.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands Splunk APM native span events in index=apm with sourcetype=apm:traces, preserving trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id, service.name, operation.name, duration fields you normalize to duration_ms at search time when nanoseconds arrive, http.method, http.url or http.route equivalents, db.system, db.statement when your privacy posture allows, and optional precomputed trace_depth or span_tree_depth when your pipeline calculates tree height at ingest. FIELDALIAS maps should collapse dotted OpenTelemetry resource attributes into stable names consumed by this search.

Lane B lands raw OpenTelemetry spans in index=otel_spans from Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector or Splunk OTel Collector Helm deployments, using the same normalization rules as Lane A so multisearch arms remain interchangeable during migrations. Preserve span.kind when you later split client versus server contributions in investigations even though this alert primarily cares about counts and depth.

Lane C lands metric rollups that already carry span_count or equivalent per trace_id, commonly sourcetype=apm:metricsets in index=metrics, prometheus:metrics lines scraped from internal trace summarizers, or otel_metric points emitted by a batch processor that counts descendants under each root. This arm prevents full rescans of multi-million span windows when Job Inspector shows elevated scan costs.

Lane D is the ITSI N plus one anti-pattern catalog delivered as a lookup itsi_apm_nplusone_antipattern_catalog.csv with columns such as pattern_service, antipattern_weight or risk_score, db_signature_hint, and optional notes for reviewers. Refresh the file when architects publish new anti-pattern classes; version the CSV in GitOps with approvals.

Lane E in narrative only: maintain apm_endpoint_span_slo_inventory.csv keyed on lowercase service_name and endpoint_key with slo_max_span_count, owner_team, environment, cluster_tier, and optional notes linking to architecture standards. The endpoint_key should match the strcat of service name, HTTP method, and a truncated URL stem used in SPL so joins are deterministic.

HTTP Event Collector tokens for trace JSON require rate monitoring; sudden client retries can explode span_count without any user traffic increase. OpenTelemetry Collector batch and attribute limit processors must be change-controlled because silent drops of parent_span_id collapse trees and distort depth math. Clock skew between services inflates perceived depth when timestamps mis-order spans; keep NTP discipline within one second on interactive paths.

Pre-save validation: pick a known mega-trace from Splunk APM Trace Analyzer, query index=apm for that trace_id, and confirm span_count in Splunk matches the UI within sampling disclosures. Validate index=otel_spans parity for the same trace when dual-writing. Validate metric lane rows by comparing metric_span_count to a manual stats count for a handful of trace_id values in a lab window.

Dashboards: publish a heatmap of span_count by endpoint_key, a histogram of unique_services_in_trace, and a table of duplicate-span ratio suspects with drilldowns to Splunk APM waterfalls and Splunk Observability Cloud trace detail.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_5_6_trace_shape_fanout_depth with a fifteen-minute schedule in steady production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is page or warn after minimum span_count guards so sparse sampling never pages alone. Throttle duplicate trace_id rows for sixty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Include recommended_action, owner_team, endpoint_key, and span_count in pager narratives so responders open ORM query plans, recursion limits, and choreography designs without re-deriving the detection math.

Pipeline narrative unique to trace shape: multisearch fans Splunk APM spans, OpenTelemetry raw spans, per-trace metric rollups, and a catalog arm used again in joins so ITSI knowledge is never silently absent when the lookup file updates between runs. coalesce ladders absorb field naming drift across bridges. stats collapses to one row per trace_id with span_count preferring metric lane values when present. eventstats computes percentile tails for global and per-endpoint populations to catch 99th percentile style cardinality outliers without hard-coding one threshold for every route. streamstats supplies short hysteresis on span_count deltas per endpoint_key to dampen single-trace flicker during retries. join to itsi_apm_nplusone_antipattern_catalog.csv raises severity when statistical fanout aligns with cataloged relational access signatures. join to apm_endpoint_span_slo_inventory.csv enforces business-owned slo_max_span_count per endpoint. join to tstats against the Splunk CIM Performance datamodel supplies a coarse perf_service_rows anchor proving the search executed in tenants with accelerated summaries, not to assert causality. case maps combined fanout, depth, service cardinality, duplicate span clusters, SLO breaches, and ITSI weights into page versus warn versus info tiers aligned to platform risk appetite.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.6 trace shape fanout depth N+1 runaway chain anti-patterns. Tunables: fanout_page=500 depth_page=25 svc_warn=15 dup_ratio=8 p99_mult=1.25 earliest=-4h@h latest=now")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,spanId,"")
      | eval parent_span_id=coalesce(parent_span_id,parentSpanId,"")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval operation_name=toString(coalesce('operation.name',operation_name,name,"unspecified_operation"))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round(duration_nano/1000000,4),round(duration_ns/1000000,4),0))
      | eval http_method=toString(coalesce(http.method,http_method,""))
      | eval http_url=toString(coalesce(http.url,http_route,http_target,""))
      | eval db_statement=toString(coalesce('db.statement',db_statement,""))
      | eval db_system=toString(coalesce('db.system',db_system,""))
      | eval trace_depth=tonumber(coalesce(trace_depth,span_tree_depth,max_child_depth,0),10)
      | eval endpoint_key=strcat(service_name,"|",http_method,"|",substr(http_url,1,160))
      | where len(trace_id)>=8 AND len(service_name)>0
      | eval lane="apm_traces_lane"
      | fields _time trace_id span_id parent_span_id service_name operation_name duration_ms http_method http_url db_statement db_system trace_depth endpoint_key lane ]
    [ search index=otel_spans earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,spanId,"")
      | eval parent_span_id=coalesce(parent_span_id,parentSpanId,"")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval operation_name=toString(coalesce('operation.name',operation_name,name,"otel_op"))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round(duration_nano/1000000,4),0))
      | eval http_method=toString(coalesce(http.method,http_method,""))
      | eval http_url=toString(coalesce(http.url,http_route,""))
      | eval db_statement=toString(coalesce('db.statement',db_statement,""))
      | eval db_system=toString(coalesce('db.system',db_system,""))
      | eval trace_depth=tonumber(coalesce(trace_depth,0),10)
      | eval endpoint_key=strcat(service_name,"|",http_method,"|",substr(http_url,1,160))
      | where len(trace_id)>=8 AND len(service_name)>0
      | eval lane="otel_spans_lane"
      | fields _time trace_id span_id parent_span_id service_name operation_name duration_ms http_method http_url db_statement db_system trace_depth endpoint_key lane ]
    [ search index=metrics (sourcetype=apm:metricsets OR sourcetype=otel_metric OR sourcetype=prometheus:metrics) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval metric_span_count=tonumber(coalesce(span_count,spanCount,metric_value,_value,"0"),10)
      | where len(trace_id)>=8 AND metric_span_count>0
      | eval lane="trace_metric_lane"
      | eval service_name="metric_only_row"
      | eval operation_name="metric_rollup"
      | eval duration_ms=0
      | eval http_method=""
      | eval http_url=""
      | eval db_statement=""
      | eval db_system=""
      | eval trace_depth=0
      | eval endpoint_key=strcat("metric|",trace_id)
      | eval span_id=trace_id
      | eval parent_span_id=""
      | fields _time trace_id span_id parent_span_id service_name operation_name duration_ms http_method http_url db_statement db_system trace_depth endpoint_key lane metric_span_count ]
    [ | inputlookup itsi_apm_nplusone_antipattern_catalog.csv
      | eval lane="itsi_nplusone_catalog"
      | eval pattern_service_key=lower(trim(toString(pattern_service)))
      | eval itsi_nplusone_score=tonumber(coalesce(antipattern_weight,risk_score,"0"),10)
      | eval itsi_db_signature=toString(coalesce(db_signature_hint,db_fingerprint,""))
      | fields lane pattern_service_key itsi_nplusone_score itsi_db_signature ]
| where match(lane,"apm_traces_lane|otel_spans_lane|trace_metric_lane")
| stats count AS span_events dc(span_id) AS dc_spans dc(service_name) AS unique_services_in_trace max(trace_depth) AS trace_depth max(metric_span_count) AS metric_span_count max(duration_ms) AS duration_ms dc(operation_name) AS dc_operations values(http_method) AS http_method values(http_url) AS http_url values(db_system) AS db_system values(db_statement) AS db_statement values(service_name) AS svc_mv values(endpoint_key) AS ep_mv BY trace_id
| eval span_count=round(coalesce(metric_span_count,dc_spans,span_events,0),0)
| eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(mvindex(mvdedup(svc_mv),0))))
| eval endpoint_key=toString(mvindex(mvdedup(ep_mv),0))
| eval http_method=toString(mvindex(mvdedup(http_method),0))
| eval http_url=toString(mvindex(mvdedup(http_url),0))
| eval db_system=toString(mvindex(mvdedup(db_system),0))
| eval db_statement=substr(toString(mvindex(mvdedup(db_statement),0)),1,240)
| eval dup_span_cluster=if(dc_operations>0 AND span_count>=(dc_operations*8) AND span_count>=24,1,0)
| eventstats perc99(span_count) AS p99_span_global
| eventstats perc99(span_count) AS p99_span_endpoint BY endpoint_key
| sort 0 + service_name + trace_id
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f last(span_count) AS prev_span_count BY endpoint_key
| eval span_burst_delta=coalesce(span_count,0)-coalesce(prev_span_count,0)
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [| inputlookup itsi_apm_nplusone_antipattern_catalog.csv
     | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(pattern_service)))
     | eval itsi_nplusone_score=tonumber(coalesce(antipattern_weight,risk_score,"0"),10)
     | fields service_name itsi_nplusone_score ]
| join type=left max=0 service_name endpoint_key
    [| inputlookup apm_endpoint_span_slo_inventory.csv
     | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(service_name)))
     | eval endpoint_key=coalesce(endpoint_key,route_key,http_route_key,"")
     | fields service_name endpoint_key slo_max_span_count owner_team environment cluster_tier ]
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS perf_service_rows FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.process
     | rename Performance.process AS service_name
     | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(service_name))) ]
| fillnull value=0 p99_span_global p99_span_endpoint itsi_nplusone_score perf_service_rows dup_span_cluster trace_depth unique_services_in_trace
| eval slo_max_span_count=tonumber(coalesce(slo_max_span_count,999999),10)
| eval severity=case(
    span_count>=500 AND itsi_nplusone_score>=2, "page",
    span_count>=500, "page",
    trace_depth>=25, "page",
    unique_services_in_trace>=15, "warn",
    dup_span_cluster=1, "warn",
    span_count>slo_max_span_count, "warn",
    span_count>=(1.25*p99_span_endpoint) AND p99_span_endpoint>=80 AND span_count>=(p99_span_endpoint+50), "warn",
    span_count>=(1.25*p99_span_global) AND p99_span_global>=120, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity="page" AND span_count>=500 AND itsi_nplusone_score>=2, "profile_orm_batch_fetch_eliminate_per_row_db_children_review_itsi_catalog_signature",
    severity="page" AND trace_depth>=25, "inspect_recursive_calls_cycle_breakers_and_async_continuation_depth",
    severity="page", "open_trace_waterfall_count_child_spans_under_root_identify_fanout_origin_operation",
    severity="warn" AND unique_services_in_trace>=15, "architecture_review_service_decomposition_and_choreography_versus_chatty_sync_chain",
    severity="warn" AND dup_span_cluster=1, "check_double_instrumentation_retry_storms_idempotent_span_names",
    severity="warn" AND span_count>slo_max_span_count, "right_size_endpoint_slo_max_span_count_and_sampling_with_owner_team",
    severity="warn", "compare_endpoint_histogram_to_p99_baseline_reduce_chatty_internal_spans",
    true(), "continue_shape_observation")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned_apm")
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown")
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier,"unrated")
| table trace_id service_name endpoint_key span_count trace_depth unique_services_in_trace http_method http_url db_system duration_ms severity recommended_action itsi_nplusone_score slo_max_span_count owner_team perf_service_rows
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_5_6_trace_shape_fanout_depth_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_5_6_trace_shape_fanout_depth | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance guidance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time exceeding internal SLOs, materialize per-trace rollups into a summary index every five minutes keyed on trace_id and endpoint_key, widen operational alerts to read summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For very large fleets, split alerts by cluster_tier from inventory.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Splunk Observability Cloud Trace Analyzer or Splunk APM trace views for a trace you already suspect is bushy. Compare visual span counts to the Splunk span_count column for the same trace_id within the same sampling disclosure. When numbers disagree, verify dual export, deduplication rules, and metric lane staleness.

Validate field normalization by sampling twenty random spans in Search and confirming duration_ms, service.name, operation.name, http.method, http.url, db.system, and db.statement resolve through the same coalesce chain used in production macros. Empty service keys usually indicate mapping drift, not honest microservice simplicity.

Validate inventory joins by running inputlookup apm_endpoint_span_slo_inventory.csv alone and confirming tier-one checkout routes include slo_max_span_count and owner_team. Validate ITSI catalog rows by confirming pattern_service keys align to normalized lowercase services.

Validate tstats join behavior by confirming the Performance datamodel accelerates in the tenant; if summariesonly=true returns zero rows, remove acceleration gating or replace with a metadata lookup until summaries build.

Synthetic positive test: in a non-production service, intentionally enable a diagnostic loop that issues per-row database calls for several hundred rows, confirm page severity within two buckets, then remove the loop and confirm decay. Synthetic negative test: on a calm window with representative traffic, filtering severity to page and warn should return zero rows for forty-five consecutive runs when thresholds are calibrated and sampling is stable.

Runbook linkage: attach internal guides for Hibernate batch sizing, Entity Framework Include strategies, SQLAlchemy joinedload, Django select_related and prefetch_related, JDBC batching, and gRPC server-side streaming refactors so engineers translate alerts into code-level fixes.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Single REST handler shows span_count above five hundred with hundreds of identical db.statement fragments and itsi_nplusone_score elevated: profile the ORM, convert eager loading to batched queries, validate database indexes on join columns, and capture before-and-after span_count in Splunk APM.

Case 2 — GraphQL resolver fanout creates one child field span per row without a relational smell: introduce DataLoader style batching, cap resolver depth, and document intentional fanout in apm_endpoint_span_slo_inventory.csv notes so alerts downgrade appropriately.

Case 3 — trace_depth crosses twenty-five on a payment orchestration service: inspect recursive refund or reversal helpers, add maximum depth guards, and verify asynchronous continuations are not re-entrant across the same trace context.

Case 4 — unique_services_in_trace exceeds fifteen on a nominally simple mobile API: challenge synchronous choreography, consolidate edge aggregation, and map the chain against architecture decision records before adding another microservice.

Case 5 — dup_span_cluster flags true after a SDK upgrade: compare instrumentation scope names, remove duplicate auto-instrumentation plus manual spans, and verify retry policies are not emitting identical client spans on every backoff tick.

Case 6 — Metric lane span_count disagrees wildly with raw span_events: rebuild the rollup exporter, reconcile tail sampling, and pause paging on metric-only anomalies until parity returns.

Case 7 — Kubernetes liveness storms generate short traces with artificially high span_burst_delta: correlate pod restart timestamps and suppress known noisy node pools via cluster_tier filters in inventory.

Case 8 — Message consumer batch processing legitimately opens hundreds of spans per chunk: raise slo_max_span_count for that endpoint_key, tag spans with workflow identifiers, and separate interactive from batch inventories.

Case 9 — Feature flag enables a shadow dependency that adds dozens of synchronous HTTP calls: treat as architecture review, not pure incident, until span_count breaches SLO; capture flag audit evidence alongside traces.

Case 10 — Partial parent_span_id drops after a mesh upgrade mimic runaway depth: stabilize UC-13.5.3 completeness signals before trusting depth alerts; compare raw parent links in index=otel_spans against index=apm.

Case 11 — Developer runs a load test without tagging environment: production-like span shapes appear in shared indexes; require deployment.environment separation and lookup annotations before paging tier-zero teams.

Case 12 — Observability entitlement pressure triggers aggressive head sampling that hides mega-traces: pair this UC with sampling policy reviews and cost dashboards so shape detection remains statistically meaningful.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists four arms including Splunk APM spans, OpenTelemetry spans, metric rollups, and ITSI catalog seed rows; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, and tstats appear; final table lists fourteen analyst-visible columns for investigations.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.5.6 trace shape fanout depth N+1 runaway chain anti-patterns. Tunables: fanout_page=500 depth_page=25 svc_warn=15 dup_ratio=8 p99_mult=1.25 earliest=-4h@h latest=now")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,spanId,"")
      | eval parent_span_id=coalesce(parent_span_id,parentSpanId,"")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval operation_name=toString(coalesce('operation.name',operation_name,name,"unspecified_operation"))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round(duration_nano/1000000,4),round(duration_ns/1000000,4),0))
      | eval http_method=toString(coalesce(http.method,http_method,""))
      | eval http_url=toString(coalesce(http.url,http_route,http_target,""))
      | eval db_statement=toString(coalesce('db.statement',db_statement,""))
      | eval db_system=toString(coalesce('db.system',db_system,""))
      | eval trace_depth=tonumber(coalesce(trace_depth,span_tree_depth,max_child_depth,0),10)
      | eval endpoint_key=strcat(service_name,"|",http_method,"|",substr(http_url,1,160))
      | where len(trace_id)>=8 AND len(service_name)>0
      | eval lane="apm_traces_lane"
      | fields _time trace_id span_id parent_span_id service_name operation_name duration_ms http_method http_url db_statement db_system trace_depth endpoint_key lane ]
    [ search index=otel_spans earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,spanId,"")
      | eval parent_span_id=coalesce(parent_span_id,parentSpanId,"")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval operation_name=toString(coalesce('operation.name',operation_name,name,"otel_op"))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round(duration_nano/1000000,4),0))
      | eval http_method=toString(coalesce(http.method,http_method,""))
      | eval http_url=toString(coalesce(http.url,http_route,""))
      | eval db_statement=toString(coalesce('db.statement',db_statement,""))
      | eval db_system=toString(coalesce('db.system',db_system,""))
      | eval trace_depth=tonumber(coalesce(trace_depth,0),10)
      | eval endpoint_key=strcat(service_name,"|",http_method,"|",substr(http_url,1,160))
      | where len(trace_id)>=8 AND len(service_name)>0
      | eval lane="otel_spans_lane"
      | fields _time trace_id span_id parent_span_id service_name operation_name duration_ms http_method http_url db_statement db_system trace_depth endpoint_key lane ]
    [ search index=metrics (sourcetype=apm:metricsets OR sourcetype=otel_metric OR sourcetype=prometheus:metrics) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval metric_span_count=tonumber(coalesce(span_count,spanCount,metric_value,_value,"0"),10)
      | where len(trace_id)>=8 AND metric_span_count>0
      | eval lane="trace_metric_lane"
      | eval service_name="metric_only_row"
      | eval operation_name="metric_rollup"
      | eval duration_ms=0
      | eval http_method=""
      | eval http_url=""
      | eval db_statement=""
      | eval db_system=""
      | eval trace_depth=0
      | eval endpoint_key=strcat("metric|",trace_id)
      | eval span_id=trace_id
      | eval parent_span_id=""
      | fields _time trace_id span_id parent_span_id service_name operation_name duration_ms http_method http_url db_statement db_system trace_depth endpoint_key lane metric_span_count ]
    [ | inputlookup itsi_apm_nplusone_antipattern_catalog.csv
      | eval lane="itsi_nplusone_catalog"
      | eval pattern_service_key=lower(trim(toString(pattern_service)))
      | eval itsi_nplusone_score=tonumber(coalesce(antipattern_weight,risk_score,"0"),10)
      | eval itsi_db_signature=toString(coalesce(db_signature_hint,db_fingerprint,""))
      | fields lane pattern_service_key itsi_nplusone_score itsi_db_signature ]
| where match(lane,"apm_traces_lane|otel_spans_lane|trace_metric_lane")
| stats count AS span_events dc(span_id) AS dc_spans dc(service_name) AS unique_services_in_trace max(trace_depth) AS trace_depth max(metric_span_count) AS metric_span_count max(duration_ms) AS duration_ms dc(operation_name) AS dc_operations values(http_method) AS http_method values(http_url) AS http_url values(db_system) AS db_system values(db_statement) AS db_statement values(service_name) AS svc_mv values(endpoint_key) AS ep_mv BY trace_id
| eval span_count=round(coalesce(metric_span_count,dc_spans,span_events,0),0)
| eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(mvindex(mvdedup(svc_mv),0))))
| eval endpoint_key=toString(mvindex(mvdedup(ep_mv),0))
| eval http_method=toString(mvindex(mvdedup(http_method),0))
| eval http_url=toString(mvindex(mvdedup(http_url),0))
| eval db_system=toString(mvindex(mvdedup(db_system),0))
| eval db_statement=substr(toString(mvindex(mvdedup(db_statement),0)),1,240)
| eval dup_span_cluster=if(dc_operations>0 AND span_count>=(dc_operations*8) AND span_count>=24,1,0)
| eventstats perc99(span_count) AS p99_span_global
| eventstats perc99(span_count) AS p99_span_endpoint BY endpoint_key
| sort 0 + service_name + trace_id
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f last(span_count) AS prev_span_count BY endpoint_key
| eval span_burst_delta=coalesce(span_count,0)-coalesce(prev_span_count,0)
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [| inputlookup itsi_apm_nplusone_antipattern_catalog.csv
     | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(pattern_service)))
     | eval itsi_nplusone_score=tonumber(coalesce(antipattern_weight,risk_score,"0"),10)
     | fields service_name itsi_nplusone_score ]
| join type=left max=0 service_name endpoint_key
    [| inputlookup apm_endpoint_span_slo_inventory.csv
     | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(service_name)))
     | eval endpoint_key=coalesce(endpoint_key,route_key,http_route_key,"")
     | fields service_name endpoint_key slo_max_span_count owner_team environment cluster_tier ]
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS perf_service_rows FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.process
     | rename Performance.process AS service_name
     | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(service_name))) ]
| fillnull value=0 p99_span_global p99_span_endpoint itsi_nplusone_score perf_service_rows dup_span_cluster trace_depth unique_services_in_trace
| eval slo_max_span_count=tonumber(coalesce(slo_max_span_count,999999),10)
| eval severity=case(
    span_count>=500 AND itsi_nplusone_score>=2, "page",
    span_count>=500, "page",
    trace_depth>=25, "page",
    unique_services_in_trace>=15, "warn",
    dup_span_cluster=1, "warn",
    span_count>slo_max_span_count, "warn",
    span_count>=(1.25*p99_span_endpoint) AND p99_span_endpoint>=80 AND span_count>=(p99_span_endpoint+50), "warn",
    span_count>=(1.25*p99_span_global) AND p99_span_global>=120, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity="page" AND span_count>=500 AND itsi_nplusone_score>=2, "profile_orm_batch_fetch_eliminate_per_row_db_children_review_itsi_catalog_signature",
    severity="page" AND trace_depth>=25, "inspect_recursive_calls_cycle_breakers_and_async_continuation_depth",
    severity="page", "open_trace_waterfall_count_child_spans_under_root_identify_fanout_origin_operation",
    severity="warn" AND unique_services_in_trace>=15, "architecture_review_service_decomposition_and_choreography_versus_chatty_sync_chain",
    severity="warn" AND dup_span_cluster=1, "check_double_instrumentation_retry_storms_idempotent_span_names",
    severity="warn" AND span_count>slo_max_span_count, "right_size_endpoint_slo_max_span_count_and_sampling_with_owner_team",
    severity="warn", "compare_endpoint_histogram_to_p99_baseline_reduce_chatty_internal_spans",
    true(), "continue_shape_observation")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned_apm")
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown")
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier,"unrated")
| table trace_id service_name endpoint_key span_count trace_depth unique_services_in_trace http_method http_url db_system duration_ms severity recommended_action itsi_nplusone_score slo_max_span_count owner_team perf_service_rows
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance host=* earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.process
| rename Performance.process AS apm_service_key
| eval apm_service_key=lower(trim(toString(apm_service_key)))
| join type=left max=0 apm_service_key [| inputlookup apm_endpoint_span_slo_inventory.csv | eval apm_service_key=lower(trim(toString(service_name))) | fields apm_service_key slo_max_span_count owner_team]
| table apm_service_key cpu_load mem_used slo_max_span_count owner_team
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries for process keys that align to APM service.name when anchoring trace-shape investigations to underlying node capacity; refresh summaries at least nightly so tstats join arms remain summariesonly=true friendly.
visualization: Histogram of span_count with page threshold markers, heatmap of unique_services_in_trace by service_name, table of severity-colored rows with drilldowns to Splunk APM waterfalls, single-value tiles for page-count-per-endpoint, timeline of span_burst_delta for retry suspicion.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Introduction to Splunk APM
Splunk Docs — Learn about Monitoring MetricSets
Splunk Docs — Get started with Splunk APM traces
Martin Fowler — Microservices (trade-offs and when to prefer monoliths)

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate batch importers and nightly reconcilers often emit wide traces on purpose; segregate them with deployment.environment, cluster_tier, or endpoint_key notes in apm_endpoint_span_slo_inventory.csv before paging interactive on-call. GraphQL and gRPC streaming workloads can show high span_count without relational N plus one; require db.system correlation before assuming ORM guilt. Aggressive head sampling and tail sampling policy edits change which mega-traces survive; reconcile sampling dashboards before muting this control. Double instrumentation during canary SDK pairs can flag dup_span_cluster until versions converge; annotate releases and downgrade duplicate severity when expected. Partial parent_span_id loss after mesh upgrades inflates apparent depth; pair with UC-13.5.3 completeness analytics before deep recursion rollbacks. Metric rollup lag during indexer maintenance can make span_count disagree across lanes; verify index=_internal forwarder health. Load tests and chaos drills without labels can mimic production anti-patterns; require labeled environments. ITSI catalog rows are advisory; stale pattern_service keys cause false confidence—refresh GitOps-owned CSVs quarterly. Performance datamodel tstats rows map processes to services imperfectly; treat perf_service_rows as supplemental context only.
hardware: Kubernetes or VM fleets exporting OpenTelemetry traces into Splunk APM or mirrored Enterprise indexes at 9.2 or newer, with optional Splunk Observability Cloud dual-write.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.5.1 duration percentile anomaly detection, UC-13.5.2 trace error-rate paging, UC-13.5.3 trace completeness and orphan-span analytics, UC-13.5.4 dependency map drift discovery, UC-13.5.5 log-to-trace correlation coverage, UC-13.5.7 RED saturation dashboards, UC-13.5.9 RUM Core Web Vitals, or UC-13.5.16 SLO burn-rate policy. Does not automatically change sampling or service topology; it produces evidence and routing guidance for human-approved fixes.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab namespace, ingest apm:traces where one checkout handler generates more than five hundred child spans against postgres with repeating db.statement fingerprints; confirm observability_uc_13_5_6_trace_shape_fanout_depth returns page within two fifteen-minute buckets with non-null recommended_action and owner_team after inventory join.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After refactoring to batched queries, when span_count falls below five hundred for sixty minutes, unique_services_in_trace stays under fifteen, trace_depth stays under twenty-five, and duplicate-cluster logic stays quiet, confirm severities return to info and the alert wrapper emits zero rows filtering to page and warn only.

evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_5_6_trace_shape_fanout_depth with scheduled execution, exported CSV rows to a restricted observability_evidence index, and dashboard drilldowns tied to Splunk APM trace exemplars.
